subject: Sociology
title: Assimilation and Pluralism
description: A fast breakdown of Assimilation and Pluralism for builders.

hook.text: America was called a melting pot. But many groups kept their language, food, and customs. The pot didn't melt everyone.

buildup.text: Assimilation theory says immigrants gradually adopt the dominant culture and lose their original identity.

discovery.text: Pluralism argues that groups can maintain distinct identities while participating equally in the larger society.

twist.text: Assimilation was easier for white Europeans. Non-white immigrants faced barriers that blocked full integration.

climax.text: Most societies fall between the extremes. Partial assimilation and cultural retention happen simultaneously.

punchline.text: The melting pot was always more of a salad bowl.

quiz.question: What is the difference between assimilation and pluralism?
options: Assimilation absorbs cultures; pluralism maintains them
